accountage.description: Shows when and how long ago a user created their twitch account.

usage.description: Defaults to the executor if no user is provided.
Get the account age of a user.

add.description: Adds one or more 7TV emotes. This searches for the provided emote(s) and adds the first exact match.
add.extra: This is not case sensitive. Both the bot and the user require 7tv editor for this command to work.

usage.description: Add 7TV emotes by searching for them. Emote ids or urls can also be provided.
Using the `as` flag will add an emote as an alias.
Using the `from` flag will add 7TV emotes that are added in the specified channel. This is case sensitive.
Using the `in` flag will add 7TV emotes from the current channel in the specified channel. This searches for emotes if they were not found in the current channel.
Using the `case` flag will add 7TV emotes that match the exact case of the provided emote name. Note: This only checks for the first 50 results!

flags.description: Adds an emote as an alias.
Adds emotes from the channel specified, this is case sensitive.
Adds emotes in the specified channel.
Only adds emotes that match the exact case.

afk.description: Sets an AFK status, and sends a message when you're back.

usage.description: Go AFK without a status.
Go AFK with a status.
Using the `brb` or `gn` alias will send a more fitting message.
Using the `rafk` alias will continue your afk status without resetting the afk time.

always.description: Allows one or more commands to still be useable while the channel is live.

usage.description: Allow one or more commands. This will also enable all of the command's aliases while live.
Allow all commands.

asay.description: Sends a message through a different user.

usage.description: Send a message.
Using the `in` flag will send the message in the specified channel.

flags.description: The channel to send the message to.

ascii.description: Makes an ascii art of a Twitch, 7TV, BTTV or FFZ emote. This uses the first frame if the emote is animated.
ascii.extra: The emote priority in order from first to last: Twitch, channel 7TV, channel BTTV, channel FFZ, global 7TV, global BTTV, global FFZ.

usage.description: Make an ascii art.
Using the `in` alias will use emotes from the specified channel.

flags.description: Looks for emotes in the provided channel.

ban.description: Bans a user from interacting in any way with the bot in the current channel.

usage.description: Ban a user.

banphrase.description: Adds a banphrase. This will ban a first time chatter if any part of it is in their message.
banphrase.extra: Banphrases are not case sensitive. The bot requires mod for the banphrases to work.

usage.description: Get all banphrases.
Add a banphrase. Enclosing the banphrase with slashes '/' will add the banphrase as a regex pattern. This is also not case sensitive.
Remove a banphrase.
Enable a banphrase.
Disable a banphrase.

usage.description: Decode binary to text.

check.description: Shows the status of a suggestion.

choice.description: Picks a random option from the provided options.

usage.description: Pick a random option. Options must be seperated by a space.
Using the `separator` flag will use the provided character(s) as an item separator, instead of a space.
Using the `amount` flag will cap the amount of items it can choose at the provided amount.
usage.example: Sobble Talonflame Scraggy Yanmega Greavard Beautifly amount:3

flags.description: Uses the provided character(s) as an item separator instead of a space.
Chooses the provided amount of items.

usage.description: Flip a coin. Will land on either heads or tails.

command.description: Manages custom commands for the current channel.

usage.description: Commands can be added or selected with regex by enclosing the trigger with slashes '/'. They can also be selected by using the id. These should always be provided right after the subcommand.
Add a custom command. Commands using a regex pattern as trigger are not case sensitive by default.
There are a few variables that can be added to the response. A full variable list can be found at the bottom.
Get the response and settings of a command.
Edit the response of a command.
Change the trigger of a command.
Add or remove aliases for a command. If a message starts with an alias, the command will also get triggered. This does not support regex.
usage.example: alias id:1234 add hello hi hey
alias id:1234 remove hello hi hey

usage.description: Add or remove keywords for a command. If a keyword is in any part of a message (seperated by a non-word character), the command will also get triggered. This does not support regex.
usage.example: keyword id:1234 add hello hi hey
keyword id:1234 remove hello hi hey

usage.description: Change the settings for a command. Multiple options can be changed in a single message. A table of all settings can be found at the bottom.
Delete a command.

table: The second word in a users message, or the username of the user if there is no second word.
The user id of the user.
The user id of the channel.
The id of the message that triggered the custom command.
A random number between the first and second numbers (inclusive).
Executes the command provided inside of the variable.
The index of a word in the users message, the words before (exclusive) or after (inclusive) the index, or the words in an index range. (Starts at 0)
Enables/Disables case sensitivity for a custom command trigger.
Sets a channel cooldown in seconds for a custom command.
Sets a user cooldown in seconds for a custom command.
Sets a minimum role requirement for a command. Valid roles: `regular`, `subscriber`, `vip`, `moderator`, `lead-moderator`, `broadcaster`

commands.description: Gets a list of all the custom command triggers and their id's in the current channel.

configure.description: Configures or shows a personal setting.

usage.description: Get the value of one of your settings. This can also be done by using the `check` flag or by not providing a value when using one of the aliases.
Configure a setting.
Reset or delete a setting. Using the `reset` flag will have the same effect.
Using the `location` alias will configure your location. This will automatically override or set your timezone as well. Using the `hide` flag will hide your location in commands like `weather`.
Using the `timezone` alias will configure your timezone. You can not change your timezone after setting your location. A country (code) can be provided instead of a timezone.

flags.description: Resets or deletes the provided setting.
Shows the value of the provided setting.
Hides you location in commands like `weather`.

usage.description: Disable one or more commands.

notify.description: Adds or removes a notification from the current channel. This will send a message when the set condition is met. Changes the notification message if an already added notification is provided.
notify.extra: Warning: If the bot is neither vip or mod in the channel, the message might not get sent due to rate limits.

usage.description: Add a notificaton. Some types of notifications also require a condition to be specified. An optional message can be added to be sent with the notification.
Update the message for an existing notification. Providing no new message will remove the previous notification message.
Using the `unnotify` or `unannounce` alias will remove an existing notification.
The available notification types are:
live: Sends a message when the provided channel goes live.
offline: Sends a message when the provided channel goes offline.
twitch-badges, twitch-emotes, 7tv-emotes, bttv-emotes, ffz-emotes: Sends a notification when a new badge/emote gets added to a platform.
usage.example: twitch-badges Time to waste 6$ for colored pixels next to my name!!

vanish.description: Vanish from the chat. This will time you out for 1 second.

usage.description: Vanish.

weather.description: Shows the weather for a location or a user. A user's location can be set by using the `configure` command.

usage.description: Get your own weather.
Get the weather for a location.
Using the `user` parameter will get the weather for the provided user.

Using the `hours` or `days` parameters will get the weather in the provided amount of days or hours. Negative amounts can be provided and these flags can be stacked (`days:-2 hours:5` would get the weather from 43 hours ago).

Using the `heat-index`, `windchill`, `dewpoint`, `gust`, `aqi`, `astronomy` and `full-condition` flag will include more in the response. Read a flag's description for more details about what it includes. The `full-temp` alias can be used to have the effect of using all 3. The `full` flag can be used to have the effect of all of these flags. Each flag adds 2-5 seconds to the cooldown.
Using the `weekweather` or `ww` alias will get the condition and temperature for the current day and the coming 7 days. Every flag that would include extra data has no effect when using this alias.

flags.description: Checks someone else's weather.
Shows the weather in the provided amount of hours.
Has the effect of using all of the following flags.
Has the effect of using the `heat-index`, `windchill` and `dewpoint` flags.
Includes the heat index in the response.
Includes the windchill in the response.
Includes the dewpoint in the response.
Includes the gust in the response.
Includes the full air quality details in the response. This only works for the current weather.
Inclues astronomy data in the response. This only works for day weather when using the `days` param without the `hours` param.
Includes the condition name instead of just the emoji(s) in the response.
